content.claim: Approximately 85% of Hungarian students attend schools with academic performance-based or recommendation-based student selection, compared to an OECD average of 43%
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Figure captures all selective-admission mechanisms (standard 4-year gimnáziums, vocational tracks, entrance tests), not solely 6/8-year extended tracks
Does not establish causal impact of selection on student outcomes
Provides comparative magnitude but not distribution across track types
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
